items.mistake: Plotting hundreds of thousands of points with default SVG Scatter
items.fix: Subsample for overview plots, aggregate (hexbin/2D histogram), or use go.Scattergl / px.scatter with render_mode='webgl' for large n.
items.why: SVG traces slow the browser and can freeze notebooks; interactivity degrades before insight appears.
items.example: For 200k training rows, plot a 5k stratified sample in px.scatter, or use px.density_heatmap for overview density.

items.mistake: Using Graph Objects for simple DataFrame scatter when Express suffices
items.fix: Start with px.scatter(data=df, x=..., y=..., color=..., hover_data=...) and drop to GO only when Express lacks a needed trace type.
items.why: Unnecessary GO boilerplate slows iteration and hides Express encodings like color and facet_col.
items.example: Prefer px.scatter(df, x='f1', y='f2', color='label') over manual go.Scatter per class loop unless mixing trace types.

items.mistake: Omitting hover metadata on ML diagnostic scatters
items.fix: Pass hover_data in Express or customdata + hovertemplate in GO so each point reveals id, true label, prediction, or split.
items.why: Without hover, interactive zoom only shows coordinates—users cannot trace outliers back to rows for relabeling or error analysis.
items.example: hover_data=['customer_id', 'y_true', 'y_pred'] on a validation feature scatter.

items.mistake: Expecting write_image PNG to preserve zoom and hover
items.fix: Use write_html for interactive sharing; reserve write_image (with Kaleido installed) for static thumbnails only.
items.why: Raster export is a snapshot; tooltips and legend toggling exist only in HTML or live Dash views.
items.example: Share run_comparison.html for the team; embed a PNG preview in slides only as a non-interactive teaser.
